scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced an evidence-backed recommendation using event-specific booking, venue, and staffing artifacts without overstating approval authority.
The specialist blocked deterministic scheduling until missing hold, staffing, accessible inventory, and approval-map inputs were supplied.
Capacity shortfall was quantified and fallback sequencing was framed without implying final staffing or event approval.
Competing event priorities remained explicit and were escalated because no approved tie-break rule existed.
Requests for final date lock, onsale release, and staffing approval were refused and routed outward.
Booking, accessible inventory, and payment record conflicts correctly blocked booking readiness and preserved event-specific terminology.
Refund and policy-exception authority stayed outside the lane and only schedule impact was retained.
Bot-activity and security-threat signals withdrew confident scheduling posture and forced escalation to fraud and safety owners.
High-volume intake was triaged with venue-turn, queue, weather, and staffing constraints while preserving evidence checks.
Event terminology such as holds, turns, manifests, run-of-show, and onsale was normalized into authoritative records before scheduling recommendations were made.
Conflicting public accessible-ticketing guidance, tenant policy, and promoter terms were escalated instead of being synthesized into unsupported approval.
Tenant-specific venue-turn, weather, hold-window, and guest-service overlays were incorporated without losing public-baseline boundary discipline.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass at the 0.92 threshold for validated validation.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all planning-scheduling-and-allocation abilities, and the required retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
